Rythmos® High Accuracy Radar Sensor

Technical Specification & Installation Reference · Rev 2026 · USB-C Powered Configuration

Fall Detection
Breathing Monitoring
Rest Quality
Behavioral Insights

Overview

Rythmos® High Accuracy Radar Sensor is a wireless sensor for in-apartment fall detection to deliver high accuracy real-time alerts via Care Manager.

FUNCTIONS

Delivers near-perfect fall detection accuracy
Provides instant caregiver notification
Reduces false alarms by 98.3%

Key Capabilities

Fall
Detection

Accuracy
for standing, sitting, and lying

Breathing
Monitoring

Subtle
respiration detection

Real-Time
Alerts

Instant
notifications via app

Behavioral
Insights

Activity
data for early intervention

Rest
Quality

Monitoring
of stillness and sleep quality

App
Integration

Feeds
the centralized CM platform

Hardware Specifications

Dimensions: 106 x 68 x 78 mm
Field of View: 120°
Coverage Area: 6 m x 6 m
Network: WiFi Connectivity
False Alarm Reduction: 98.3%
Firmware Updates: Over-the-air (OTA)
Radar Frequency: 60 GHz
Elevation: 60°
Power Source: 5V DC Micro USB (cable included)
Fall Detection Accuracy: ~100%
Indicator: LED light indicator
Installation: Indoor, corner mount

Network & Location Features

Fall Detection Accuracy: Near-100% accuracy for standing, sitting, lying, and sudden drops

Machine Learning: Algorithms continuously improve detection over time

False Alarm Reduction: 98.3% reduction versus traditional PIR or camera-based systems

Field of View: 120° horizontal field of view with 60° elevation angle

Micro-movement Monitoring: Accuracy down to 0.2 mm for breathing rate, stillness, and rest quality

Coverage Area: Up to 6 m x 6 m per unit, suitable for standard senior living apartments

System Topology — High Accuracy Radar Sensor Deployment

Mount the Radar Sensor in a corner of the room, near the ceiling, for optimal 6 m x 6 m coverage across the space. Corner mounting maximizes the 120° FOV to cover the full room from a single unit. The 60° elevation angle ensures complete floor-level detection for fall events. For rooms larger than 6 m x 6 m, use multiple sensors with overlapping coverage zones. The sensor is privacy-safe—no camera, no images, no video at any time.

Installation Guidelines

Placement

- Corner mount at ceiling height for full 6 m x 6 m coverage.

Coverage Density

- Standard senior apartments typically require a single unit

Power Routing

- Powered via Micro USB 5V DC; nearest power source required

Privacy & Compliance

- Fully privacy-safe for residents; OTA updates managed via the portal

intrexis.com
